execution_summary: Built a Python 3.10 Docker image with numpy/pandas/scipy, started the container, and executed the Python rewrite entrypoint (replication_data/analysis__py.py). Initial run failed due to the script reading input from /app/data while the code/data are mounted under /workspace; I patched the script to read from /workspace and reran successfully. The analysis computed subject-level 1D scores (max of two 1D-consistent rules) and performed an equal-variance two-sample t-test comparing contingent vs full-information conditions. Results showed a positive mean difference favoring contingent feedback with a 95% CI excluding zero, consistent with the preregistered focal claim (original CI [.03, .14]).

code_executed.logs: FileNotFoundError: Expected data file not found at /app/data/replication_data/analysis-data.csv. Patched script to load from /workspace/replication_data/analysis-data.csv.

n_subjects_inferred=474; contingent n=251, mean=0.7708, sd=0.1847; full-information n=223, mean=0.6974, sd=0.1317; mean_diff=0.07334; t=4.920; df=472; p=1.20e-06; SE=0.01491; 95% CI [0.0441, 0.1026]; Cohen's d=0.453; bootstrap 95% CI [0.0444, 0.1016]. Artifacts written to /app/data/replication_test_summary.csv and /app/data/replication_results.json.

results.hypothesis_tested: Participants in the contingent feedback condition will adopt an inferior one-dimension (1D) categorization strategy more often than participants in the full-information condition (i.e., higher 1D scores under contingent feedback).

findings_summary.outcome_name: t-statistic for difference in acc_1d (equal variances)

tables.table_description: Group summary of acc_1d by condition (mean, SD, N) at the subject level.
Main analysis results (JSON): group stats, mean difference, t, p, SE, CI, Cohen's d, bootstrap CI.
